title: vaccinium oxycoccos var. microphyllum
intro: Vaccinium oxycoccos var. microphyllum, also known as low-bush cranberry, is a low-growing shrub species within the Vaccinium genus, native to northern parts of North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant's berries have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as diarrhea, digestive problems, and urinary tract infections, providing relief and promoting overall well-being. The low-bush cranberry can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions.
